  "findings": [
    "Double-tapped circuit breakers that are not listed or identified for more than one conductor."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove the extra conductors from the non-listed circuit breakers.",
    "Install a listed distribution block, use pig-tails, or replace the breakers with types listed for two conductors to resolve the double-tap."
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC 110.14(A) states that terminals for more than one conductor shall be so identified. Standard circuit breakers are typically only listed for a single conductor unless explicitly marked otherwise. Double-tapping non-listed breakers creates a fire and safety hazard due to loose connections.",
  "references": [
    "NEC Art. 110.14(A)",
    "NEC Art. 408"
  ]
}
